_note: Management-consulting craft criteria. The measurement function is given by each `algorithm` field — MECE completeness (mece-axes·mece-clusters) uses `eval_*_aggregate_mece`; numeric density (numeric-density) uses `count_density_violations`. judge=M (SCR·bold-bullet·so-what·numeric precision — executive-summary narrative) keeps only definition + pass_condition and is reviewed qualitatively by desk. Authoring/review prose (with examples) is in SKILL.md. Shared parsing is orchestrator-injected. Any Korean literals are the wiki's actual section headers.

con.scr.name: SCR structure
con.scr.pass_condition: The intro shows all three of Situation·Complication·Resolution.
con.scr.source: How to Write an Executive Summary Like McKinsey — Slideworks

con.so-what.name: So-what upfront
con.so-what.pass_condition: The intro leads with the conclusion (key implication) rather than dragging through description/preamble.
con.so-what.source: How to Write an Executive Summary Like McKinsey — Slideworks

con.bold-bullet.name: Bold-bullet pattern
con.bold-bullet.pass_condition: Metric runs are consistently built as a **bold key-claim heading** + supporting bullets.
con.bold-bullet.source: Understanding BCG's Approach to Executive Summaries — Insight7

con.numeric-precision.name: Numeric precision
con.numeric-precision.pass_condition: Concrete numbers (amount·date·%·proper noun) support claims instead of vague quantifiers (e.g. 'a large amount'·'trillions').
con.numeric-precision.source: How to Write an Executive Summary Like McKinsey — Slideworks

con.numeric-density.name: Numeric density (BCG isolation)
con.numeric-density.source: Understanding BCG's Approach to Executive Summaries — Insight7
con.numeric-density.note: Number of paragraphs whose number-token count > max_per_para is ≤ threshold (0). Key numbers isolated in bold-bullets. max_per_para·threshold injected by manifest.

con.mece-axes.name: Tension-axis grouping (MECE)
con.mece-axes.note: 2–4 ### <axis> subsections under ## Per-Theme Deep Analysis, every theme under ≥1 axis (the residual '기타'/other axis excluded). Collectively Exhaustive.

con.mece-clusters.name: Cluster-section completeness (MECE)
con.mece-clusters.note: Every cluster in graph/_clusters.json appears as a ## N. [[slug|alias]] section (Collectively Exhaustive).
